skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec. Use for: programmatic video generation, animated graphics, motion design, data-driven videos, React animations to video. Triggers: remotion, render video from code, tsx to video, react video, programmatic video, remotion render, code to video, animated video, motion graphics code, react animation video
skill.summary: Convert React/Remotion TSX components into MP4 videos through the inference.sh API.
skill.author: inference-skills

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate video rendering service wrapper. The static analyzer flagged backtick characters in bash command examples as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' and documentation URLs as 'Hardcoded URL' - both are false positives. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts were also false positives from non-crypto text patterns. No actual security risks were found. The skill safely wraps the inference.sh video rendering API.

content.user_title: Render MP4 videos from React/Remotion TSX code
content.value_statement: Creating videos from code is complex and time-consuming. This skill enables programmatic video generation by accepting React/Remotion TSX components and returning rendered MP4 files through the inference.sh API.
actual_capabilities: Render React/Remotion TSX components as MP4 videos with configurable resolution up to 1920x1080
Support for Remotion APIs including useCurrentFrame, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, and Sequence
Adjustable video parameters: FPS (frames per second), duration, codec, width, and height
Pass custom props to React components for dynamic video content
Stream rendering progress for long videos
Generate animated text, counters, spring animations, and sequential compositions
limitations: Requires valid inference.sh account and authentication via belt CLI
Video rendering occurs on remote inference.sh servers - no local rendering
TSX code must export a default React component and follow Remotion constraints
Complex compositions with heavy computations may timeout on remote servers

use_cases.title: Generate animated marketing videos from data
use_cases.description: Create personalized video content for users by passing dynamic props to Remotion components. Generate promotional clips with names, stats, or metrics embedded in animated graphics.

use_cases.title: Automate data visualization videos
use_cases.description: Transform charts, graphs, and data visualizations into animated videos. Code-based generation allows version control and reproducible video output for reporting pipelines.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts and developers building reporting systems

use_cases.title: Prototype motion graphics quickly
use_cases.description: Iterate on motion graphics designs by writing TSX code instead of using GUI tools. Push code changes and receive rendered video output without manual animation work.

prompt_templates.title: Simple animated text
prompt_templates.prompt: Use remotion-render to create a 3-second video with white text 'Hello World' that fades in from 0% to 100% opacity over the full duration. Use a black background. Render at 1920x1080, 30fps.

prompt_templates.title: Dynamic counter animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use remotion-render to render a progress counter that counts from 0 to 100 over 5 seconds. Show the percentage in large white text centered on a black background. Use 60fps for smooth animation.

prompt_templates.title: Animated title sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use remotion-render to create a 4-second title sequence. First show 'First' text fading in at 0 seconds, then 'Second' fading in at 1 second, then 'Third' fading in at 2 seconds. All centered on black background.

prompt_templates.title: Personalized video with props
prompt_templates.prompt: Use remotion-render to render a video with title 'My Video' and subtitle 'Created with Remotion' passed as props. Create a centered title card with title in 80px white text and subtitle in 40px gray text below it.

output_examples.input: Render a spring animation with a white rounded box on purple background that bounces in and out over 2 seconds at 1080x1080 resolution.
output_examples.output: Returns MP4 video URL showing a white 200x200 rounded rectangle bouncing with spring physics on purple background, rendered at 60fps.

output_examples.input: Generate a 3-second fade-in welcome message with 'Welcome' text on a dark blue background.
output_examples.output: Returns MP4 video URL showing 'Welcome' text in 80px font gradually appearing over 2 seconds with dark blue (#1a1a2e) background.

best_practices: Always specify explicit dimensions (width/height), FPS, and duration_seconds for consistent output
Use the interpolate function for smooth opacity and scale transitions instead of abrupt changes
Test components with short durations (1-2 seconds) first to verify rendering works before scaling up
anti_patterns: Do not include import statements in the code parameter - imports are provided by the runtime
Avoid extremely long duration videos without streaming - the render may timeout
Do not assume external resources are available - embed all assets or use data URLs

faq.question: What is Remotion and how does it relate to this skill?
faq.answer: Remotion is a framework for creating videos using React. This skill wraps the inference.sh API to let you pass Remotion TSX component code and receive rendered MP4 output without installing Remotion locally.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need the inference.sh CLI (belt) installed and authenticated. Install instructions are available at the GitHub repository linked in the skill documentation.

faq.question: What React and Remotion features are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports all standard Remotion APIs including useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence, Audio, Video, and Img. You can also use React hooks like useState and useEffect.

faq.question: How long can videos be rendered?
faq.answer: Video duration is configurable but longer videos may timeout. For videos over 30 seconds, consider using the streaming option to monitor progress and handle partial results.

faq.question: Can I use this from Python instead of the CLI?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes Python SDK examples using the inferencesh package. You can run the same remotion-render app and stream progress updates or get final video URLs.

faq.question: What video formats and resolutions are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports configurable width and height up to at least 1920x1080. Codec can be specified in the input. Default settings produce standard MP4 output suitable for web playback.
